steps.title: Substitute the expression for t
steps.content: We have $t = p^{2n-1}$, so substitute this into the original equation $p^{\frac{4}{7}} = t^{\frac{5}{6}}$: $$p^{\frac{4}{7}} = (p^{2n-1})^{\frac{5}{6}}$$

steps.title: Apply the power rule and set exponents equal
steps.content: Use the rule $(a^m)^n = a^{mn}$ to rewrite the right side: $$p^{\frac{4}{7}} = p^{(2n-1) \cdot \frac{5}{6}}$$ Since both sides have the same base $p$ and $p > 1$, the exponents must be equal: $$\frac{4}{7} = \frac{5(2n-1)}{6}$$

steps.title: Solve for n
steps.content: Expand the right side: $$\frac{4}{7} = \frac{10n - 5}{6}$$ Cross-multiply: $$4 \cdot 6 = 7(10n - 5)$$ $$24 = 70n - 35$$ Add 35 to both sides: $$59 = 70n$$ Divide by 70: $$n = \frac{59}{70}$$
